name: Watercolour Painting
tagline: Express yourself with colour and water

seo.title: Best Beginner Watercolour Painting Kit UK 2025 | KitUp
seo.description: Start watercolour painting with the right supplies. Honest beginner recommendations for paints, brushes, and paper with Amazon UK links.
keywords: what do I need to start watercolour painting

intro: Watercolour painting is one of the most rewarding hobbies you can pick up — but buying the wrong supplies early on is frustrating. Here's exactly what to start with.

kits.label: Starter Kit
kits.description: Everything you need to start painting today, without spending a fortune.

products.name: Winsor & Newton Cotman Watercolour Set (12 Half Pans)
products.description: The most trusted beginner watercolour set in the UK. Cotman paints are student-grade but genuinely good quality — far above cheap art sets.
products.why_we_picked: Winsor & Newton Cotman is the benchmark for beginner watercolours. Consistent pigment, reliable mixing, and widely available replacements.

products.name: Arches Watercolour Paper Block (300gsm, A4)
products.description: Heavy 300gsm paper that won't buckle when wet. Proper watercolour paper makes a bigger difference than expensive paints.
products.why_we_picked: Cheap paper ruins watercolour work — it buckles, bleeds, and fights you. Arches is the professional standard and available in A4 for beginners.

products.name: Daler-Rowney Aquafine Watercolour Brush Set
products.description: A set of three essential brush shapes — round, flat, and detail. Synthetic bristles that hold water well.
products.why_we_picked: You only need three brush shapes to get started. This set covers all the basics without overwhelming you with choices.

products.name: Porcelain Palette with Deep Wells
products.description: A white porcelain palette lets you see true paint colours and is easy to clean. Deep wells stop colours mixing accidentally.
products.why_we_picked: Plastic palettes stain and make it hard to judge colours. A cheap porcelain palette lasts forever and improves your work immediately.

kits.label: Level Up Kit
kits.description: Upgrade your supplies once you've caught the watercolour bug.

products.name: Winsor & Newton Professional Watercolour Set (12 Tubes)
products.description: Artist-grade pigments with exceptional depth and lightfastness. You'll notice the difference immediately.
products.why_we_picked: When you're ready to take your work seriously, professional paints are a revelation. The colour intensity and mixing qualities are in a different league.

products.name: Masking Fluid (Winsor & Newton)
products.description: Apply masking fluid to preserve white areas before laying washes. Peel off to reveal crisp white paper beneath.
products.why_we_picked: Masking fluid unlocks a whole new level of watercolour technique. It's one of those tools that feels like a cheat code when you first use it.

checklist: Detail brush (size 2 or 4) for fine work
Two water jars (one to rinse, one clean)
Masking tape to secure paper to board

faqs.question: Pans or tubes — which is better for beginners?
faqs.answer: Pans are more convenient and less wasteful for beginners. Tubes give richer colour and are better for larger paintings. Start with a pan set and add tubes as you progress.

faqs.question: Do I need expensive paper to start?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is the one area not to skimp. Cheap paper buckles when wet and makes watercolour incredibly frustrating. 300gsm cold press is the minimum. Arches or Fabriano are worth it.

faqs.question: Can I teach myself watercolour painting?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. YouTube channels like Liron Yanconsky and The Mind of Watercolor are excellent for free tutorials. Start with simple colour mixing exercises before attempting subjects.

faqs.question: What's the difference between cold press and hot press paper?
faqs.answer: Cold press has a slight texture (tooth) that most beginners find easier to control. Hot press is smooth, which suits detailed work but is harder to use for washes. Start with cold press.
